technique: Oil on canvas (here reproduced on a playing card!)
description: Plot twist: this masterpiece is starring as the Ace of Diamonds in someone's card deck! In the painting, the gloriously confident allegory of Liberty — a bare-chested woman in a yellow dress and a red Phrygian cap — charges forward waving the French tricolor flag in one hand and a bayoneted musket in the other. Beside her, a plucky young boy brandishes pistols (he's basically the original action hero). Beneath their feet sprawl fallen revolutionaries, while a smoky, chaotic battlefield rages behind. It commemorates the July Revolution of 1830. And yes, that's a thumb covering part of it and a sock peeking in at the bottom — high art, casual setting.
ambiance: Heroic, electric and rebellious — the painting practically shouts 'Vive la liberté!' even when shrunk down to a card you'd slap on the table to win a hand.
